analysis.reasoning: Core message: A Chinese envoy states that Afghanistan’s future political arrangements should be broadly representative. This signals China advocating an inclusive, representative political outcome in another country. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (West immoral/hostile): Not referenced; no critique of the West. CH2 (China is a benevolent power): Yes—positioning China as supporting inclusive governance and a constructive, diplomatic stance aligns with portraying China as cooperative and supportive of stability and just arrangements abroad. CH3 (epic history): Not relevant. CH4 (appropriate political system/values domestically): Not about China’s own system or domestic values. CH5 (CCP governance success): Not about China’s internal achievements. CH6 (culture/nature/heritage): Not relevant. Conclusion: Only CH2 is promoted.
